summary: freertc postinstall rewrites host package.json to unpin itself to "latest" and re-runs npm install
details: On install, scripts/postinstall-message.mjs locates the host project's package.json via INIT_CWD, iterates its dependencies/devDependencies/optionalDependencies, and rewrites any freertc entry to the string "latest". It then writes the file back and invokes spawnSync('npm', ['install'], { cwd: projectRoot,... }) to immediately re-resolve dependencies. This silently strips the version pin the installer chose for freertc and replaces it with an unpinned floating reference, giving the publisher an automatic update channel into every installer's project. Any future version published under this name — including a malicious one — will be pulled into all existing installers on their next npm install, with no further action by the maintainer of the host project. The mutation is performed without the user's consent and without any opt-out, and it modifies a file (the consumer's package.json) that the package has no business touching. This is install-time tampering with the installer's source tree to establish a persistent, attacker-controlled update path.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
